mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates expanding labor income and consumption capacity, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive following month-over-month PAYEMS increases in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 7.0% signals substantial labor market slack that constrains wage-push inflation and keeps monetary policy accommodative, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (annualized Sharpe ratio below 0) during periods when UNRATE exceeds 7.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirm a synchronized recovery in consumer and residential investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of joint-up months, or the interaction term contributes no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In the post-2008 recovery regime, positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals durable demand normalization and earnings upside, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2013 real GDP vintage reading of 13746.2
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarters during 2011-2015, or the strategy’s information coefficient is not statistically positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 0.2% signals rising inflation expectations that compress real discount rates and lift nominal equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeding 0.2% do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of such months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of months, or the rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 0.5% combined with a rising consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) creates negative real interest rates that stimulate corporate borrowing and risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL is rising do not produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of qualifying periods, or the joint signal underperforms the sum of individual signals (t-test p < 0.05 for interaction necessity).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) crossing above 800 thousand units signals a durable residential investment rebound that spills over to construction employment and durable goods demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings above 800 thousand units are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.
